commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=80c73c1ebd41ff8f2b1352d9840225ff13238279)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes model fallback AbortError handling to rethrow only when the external abort signal is already aborted and adds unit coverage for user aborts versus provider-side AbortErrors with a configured fallback.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +30. Total +40 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows a provider-side AbortError with no successful fallback still reaches the last-candidate raw throw and then the generic group/channel silent-reply path; I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This PR needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for the incomplete no-fallback fix and missing real behavior proof; it is not a safe ClawSweeper repair lane while proof is still contributor-owned.

target.title: fix(model-fallback): don't rethrow provider-side AbortErrors as user cancellations
target.body:
## Problem

When the LLM API closes the connection mid-stream (e.g. a network hiccup or provider-side timeout), the fetch layer surfaces an `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` with **no external abort signal triggered**. The existing `shouldRethrowAbort` check in `runFallbackCandidate` only inspected `err.name === "AbortError"`, with no regard for whether the external `abortSignal` had actually fired.

This caused a silent failure chain:

1. Provider drops connection → `AbortError` thrown from within `run()`
2. `runFallbackCandidate` catches it → `shouldRethrowAbort` = true, `normalizedFailover` = null → **rethrows immediately**, skipping all fallback candidates
3. Error bubbles to `agent-runner-execution.ts` → not a user/restart/draining abort → falls through to `buildExternalRunFailureReply` → returns `isGenericRunnerFailure: true`
4. `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForConversation` sees a group/channel session + generic failure → returns **`SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`**
5. User receives **zero visible response**; every subsequent message in the same topic repeats the same path → **topic is permanently silenced**

## Fix

Gate the `shouldRethrowAbort` rethrow on `params.abortSignal?.aborted`:

```diff
-    if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover) {
+    // Only rethrow AbortErrors triggered by the external signal (user/gateway cancel).
+    // Provider-side AbortErrors (no signal fired) fall through to fallback/error reply.
+    if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover && params.abortSignal?.aborted) {
       throw err;
     }
```

When the abort originates inside the provider with no external signal set, the error now returns `{ ok: false }` so the fallback loop can either:
- Try an alternate model (if fallbacks are configured), or
- Surface a visible error message to the user via the normal failure path

## Tests

- Updated `"does not fall back on user aborts"` → now passes a pre-aborted `AbortController.signal`, which is the correct precondition for genuine user cancellation
- Added `"falls back when AbortError comes from the LLM provider (no external abort signal)"` — regression test covering the bug path: provider-side AbortError without an external signal falls through to the fallback candidate and returns a result

All 78 tests in `model-fallback.test.ts` pass.
target.author: shengting
